chapter_title.ar: باب الْعَصِيرِ الَّذِي قَدْ مَسَّتْهُ النَّارُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 27 – The (grape) juice which the fire has touched

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/282)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/282)
text: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ الْعَصِيرِ يُطْبَخُ بِالنَّارِ حَتَّى يَغْلِيَ مِنْ سَاعَتِهِ فَيَشْرَبُهُ صَاحِبُهُ قَالَ إِذَا تَغَيَّرَ عَنْ حَالِهِ وَ غَلَى فَلَا خَيْرَ فِيهِ حَتَّى يَذْهَبَ ثُلُثَاهُ وَ يَبْقَى ثُلُثُهُ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ibn Abu Najran, from Muhammad Bin Al Haysam,

(It has been narrated) from a man from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, said, ‘I asked him<sup>asws</sup> about the (grape) juice cooked by the fire until it boils from a time, so its owner drinks it. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘When it changes from its (original) state and boils, so there is no goodness in it until two-thirds of it evaporates and there remains a third’.
en.sarwar: 2. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn abu Najran from Muhammad ibn al-Haytham from a man who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about grape juice which is cooked on fire until it boils for one hour; then the owner drinks it. He (the Imam) said, ‘If it changes and boils, it then becomes devoid of goodness until two-thirds of it is evaporated and only one-third has remained.’”
